en.extract:
Archbold's bowerbird (Archboldia papuensis) is a passerine bird in the bowerbird family Ptilonorhynchidae that is endemic to highland forests of New Guinea.
It is medium-sized, dark grey songbird with brown iris, grey feet and black bill. The male has narrow black scalloping with golden yellow crown feathers. The female is smaller than the male, with yellow patch on the wing and has no crown feathering.


Taxonomy
Archbold's bowerbird was formally described in 1940 by the Canadian zoologist Austin L. Rand from a male specimen collected at an altitude of 2,200 m (7,200 ft) 18 km (11 mi) north of Lake Habbema in the Snow Mountains of Western New Guinea. Rand erected a new genus, Archboldia, and coined the binomial name Archboldia papuensis. The genus and common names honour the American zoologist Richard Archbold. A molecular phylogenetic study by Per Ericson and collaborators published in 2020 found that Archbold's bowerbird was embedded in the genus Amblyornis.
Two subspecies are recognised:

A. p. papuensis Rand, 1940 – montane central west New Guinea
A. p. sanfordi Mayr & Gilliard, 1950 – montane central east New Guinea
The subspecies A. p. sanfordi, Sanford's bowerbird,  has sometimes been treated as a separate species.


Description
The male Archbold's bowerbird has an overall length of 37 cm (15 in) and a weight of 170–195 g (6.0–6.9 oz). The female is slight smaller with a length of 35 cm (14 in) and a weight of 163–185 g (5.7–6.5 oz). The male of the nominate race is dark grey to black with an orange-yellow crest. The bill is black, the legs are blue-grey and the iris is dark brown. The female is dark brown rather than black and lacks a crest. The primary coverts are yellowish producing a patch on the leading edge of the wing.
Archbold's bowerbird uses the shed ornamental plumes of the King of Saxony bird-of-paradise to decorate its courtship bower.
Archbold's bowerbird is evaluated as least concern on the IUCN Red List of Threatened Species.


References


External links
BirdLife Species Factsheet

'''Archbold's bowerbird''' ('''''Archboldia papuensis''''') is a [[passerine]] bird in the bowerbird family [[Ptilonorhynchidae]] that is [[endemism in birds|endemic]] to highland forests of [[New Guinea]].

It is medium-sized, dark grey [[songbird]] with brown [[Iris (anatomy)|iris]], grey feet and black [[Beak|bill]]. The male has narrow black scalloping with golden yellow crown feathers. The female is smaller than the male, with yellow patch on the wing and has no crown feathering.

==Taxonomy==
Archbold's bowerbird was [[species description|formally described]] in 1940 by the Canadian zoologist [[Austin L. Rand]] from a male specimen collected at an altitude of {{cvt|2200|m}} {{cvt|18|km}} north of Lake Habbema in the [[Sudirman Range|Snow Mountains]] of [[Western New Guinea]]. Rand erected a new genus, ''Archboldia'', and coined the [[binomial name]] ''Archboldia papuensis''.<ref>{{ cite book | last=Rand | first=Austin L. | author-link=Austin L. Rand | date=1940 | title=Results of the Archbold Expeditions. No. 25. New Birds from the 1938-1939 expedition | series=American Museum Novitates | volume=1072 | location=New York | publisher=The American Museum of Natural History | page=9 | hdl=2246/3717 | url=http://hdl.handle.net/2246/3717 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr  | editor2-last=Greenway | editor2-first=James C. Jr | year=1962 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=15 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | location=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=175 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14485548 }}</ref> The genus and common names honour the American zoologist [[Richard Archbold]].<ref>{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=Archboldia | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=Archboldia | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=29 May 2025 }}</ref> A [[molecular phylogenetic]] study by Per Ericson and collaborators published in 2020 found that Archbold's bowerbird was embedded in the genus ''[[Amblyornis]]''.<ref>{{Cite journal | last1=Ericson | first1=P.G.P. | last2=Irestedt | first2=M. | last3=Nylander | first3=J.A.A. | last4=Christidis | first4=L. | last5=Joseph | first5=L. | last6=Qu | first6=Y. | date=2020 | title=Parallel evolution of bower-building behavior in two groups of bowerbirds suggested by phylogenomics | journal=Systematic Biology | volume=69 | issue=5 | pages=820–829 | doi=10.1093/sysbio/syaa040 | doi-access=free | pmid=32415976 | pmc=7440736 }}</ref>

Two [[subspecies]] are recognised:<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=February 2025 | title=Lyrebirds, scrubbirds, bowerbirds, Australasian treecreepers, Australasian wrens | work=IOC World Bird List Version 15.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/lyrebirds/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=30 May 2025 }}</ref>
* ''A. p. papuensis'' [[Austin L. Rand|Rand]], 1940 – montane central west [[New Guinea]]
* ''A. p. sanfordi'' [[Ernst Mayr|Mayr]] & [[Ernest Thomas Gilliard|Gilliard]], 1950 – montane central east [[New Guinea]]
The subspecies ''A. p. sanfordi'', Sanford's bowerbird,  has sometimes been treated as a separate species.<ref name=bow>{{cite journal | last1=Frith | first1=C. | last2=Frith | first2=D. | year=2020 | title=Archbold's Bowerbird (''Archboldia papuensis''), version 1.0 | editor1-last=del Hoyo | editor1-first=J. | editor2-last=Elliott | editor2-first=A. | editor3-last=Sargatal | editor3-first=J. | editor4-last=Christie | editor4-first=D.A. | editor5-last=de Juana | editor5-first=E. | journal=Birds of the World | location=Ithaca, NY, USA | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | doi=10.2173/bow.arcbow1.01 }}</ref><ref name=hbw>{{ cite book | last1=Frith | first1=C.B. | last2=Frith | first2=D.W. | year=2009 | chapter=Family Ptilonorhynchidae (Bowerbirds) | editor1-last=del Hoyo | editor1-first=J. | editor2-last=Elliott | editor2-first=A. | editor3-last=Sargatal | editor3-first=J. | title=Handbook of the Birds of the World | volume=14: Bush-shrikes to Old World Sparrows | location=Barcelona, Spain | publisher=Lynx Edicions | isbn=978-84-96553-50-7 | pages=350–403 [396] | chapter-url=https://archive.org/details/handbookofbirdso0014unse/page/396/mode/1up | chapter-url-access=registration }}</ref>

==Description==
The male Archbold's bowerbird has an overall length of {{cvt|37|cm}} and a weight of {{cvt|170|-|195|g}}. The female is slight smaller with a length of {{cvt|35|cm}} and a weight of {{cvt|163|-|185|g}}. The male of the nominate race is dark grey to black with an orange-yellow crest. The bill is black, the legs are blue-grey and the iris is dark brown. The female is dark brown rather than black and lacks a crest. The [[Covert feather|primary coverts]] are yellowish producing a patch on the leading edge of the wing.<ref name=hbw/>

Archbold's bowerbird uses the shed ornamental plumes of the [[King of Saxony bird-of-paradise]] to decorate its courtship bower.<ref name=hbw/>

Archbold's bowerbird is evaluated as [[least concern]] on the [[IUCN Red List of Threatened Species]].<ref name=iucn/>

zh.extract:
雪山园丁鸟（学名：Archboldia papuensis），也称阿氏园丁鸟，是雀形目园丁鸟科的一种鸟类。
雪山园丁鸟体重约177.6克，翼长约162毫米，嘴峰长约33.5毫米，喙宽度约8.2毫米，喙厚度约9.4毫米，跗蹠长约43.4毫米，尾长约158.3毫米。雪山园丁鸟是部分迁徙的鸟类，其栖息在密集的高大树木组成的森林中，食性为杂食性。


亚种
A. p. papuensis：分布于西新几内亚拿骚。
A. p. sanfordi：分布于新几内亚中央山脉，该物种分类地位有争议，也被视为单独的物种桑氏园丁鸟。
